subject: Optimize ImmichTo115 Vue3 Frontend UI/UX
description:
You are optimizing the frontend UI of a backup management tool called ImmichTo115. It's a Vue 3 + Naive UI + Tailwind CSS v4 project.

## Project Context
- A web app that backs up Immich photo library to 115 cloud storage via WebDAV/Rclone
- Go backend with embedded Vue3 frontend (single binary deployment)
- Chinese-language UI (keep all text in Chinese)
- Tech stack: Vue 3 (script setup), Naive UI component library, Tailwind CSS v4 (import-based, no config file), Vue Router

## Current Files (all under web/src/)
- `App.vue` — NConfigProvider + NMessageProvider wrapper
- `components/AppLayout.vue` — Sidebar layout with NLayout/NLayoutSider/NMenu
- `views/DashboardView.vue` — Status cards, backup control buttons, real-time log viewer
- `views/SetupView.vue` — 4-step wizard (WebDAV → Encrypt → Backup dirs → Cron)
- `views/RestoreView.vue` — Cloud file browser with breadcrumb navigation + data table
- `router/index.ts` — 3 routes: dashboard, setup, restore
- `api/index.ts` — Typed API client
- `composables/useWebSocket.ts` — WebSocket connection for real-time logs
- `styles/main.css` — Only contains `@import "tailwindcss";`

## What to Improve

### 1. AppLayout.vue - Sidebar & Overall Layout
- Make the sidebar look more professional and modern
- Add a proper logo/brand area at the top (can use an SVG icon or styled text)
- Improve the menu hover/active states
- Add a subtle gradient or brand color to the sidebar background
- Consider adding a footer in the sidebar with version info
- Make it responsive: on mobile, sidebar should collapse to a hamburger menu

### 2. DashboardView.vue - Dashboard
- Make status cards more visually appealing with icons, better color coding, subtle shadows
- Improve the log viewer: add line numbers, better contrast, smooth scroll animation
- Make the backup control buttons area more polished
- Add a subtle header/hero section with a summary
- Improve spacing and visual hierarchy

### 3. SetupView.vue - Setup Wizard
- Make the stepper more visually appealing
- Add subtle animations or transitions between steps
- Improve form layout with better spacing and visual grouping
- Add helpful tooltips or descriptions for each field
- Make the navigation buttons more prominent

### 4. RestoreView.vue - Cloud File Browser
- Improve the breadcrumb navigation styling
- Better empty state with an illustration or icon
- Improve the file table styling
- Add file type icons (folder, image, video, etc.)

### 5. General
- Add smooth page transitions between routes
- Improve the overall color scheme - use a cohesive palette (suggest: blue-teal gradient theme)
- Better dark mode readiness (NConfigProvider supports theme switching)
- Add subtle hover/focus effects throughout

## CRITICAL RULES
1. Keep ALL Chinese text exactly as-is, do not translate anything
2. Keep ALL existing functionality intact - this is a VISUAL-ONLY upgrade
3. Do NOT change any API calls, WebSocket logic, or router configuration
4. Do NOT change TypeScript types or interfaces
5. Do NOT add new npm dependencies - work with existing: naive-ui, tailwindcss v4, vue, vue-router
6. Do NOT modify vite.config.ts, package.json, tsconfig.json, or any config files
7. Do NOT modify api/index.ts or composables/useWebSocket.ts
8. Keep the same component structure - same files, same component names
9. After editing, run: cd web && npx vue-tsc --noEmit to verify TypeScript passes
10. After TypeScript check, run: cd web && npx vite build to verify build passes
11. The build output dir is ../cmd/server/dist (already configured in vite.config.ts)

## Build Commands
```bash
cd web
npx vue-tsc --noEmit   # TypeScript check
npx vite build          # Build (outputs to ../cmd/server/dist)
```

Make the UI look like a professional SaaS dashboard. Think: clean, modern, with good use of whitespace, subtle shadows, and smooth interactions.
